mechanism_narrative: Rising federal funds rates increase the discount rate for future equity cash flows, directly compressing present values and generating negative S&P 500 forward returns over the next month.
failure_condition: 20-day forward returns show positive mean or Rank IC > 0.05 during periods of rising FEDFUNDS.

mechanism_narrative: A sharp monthly increase in real spending on financial services signals accelerating consumer demand and liquidity injection, which drives immediate positive equity price momentum over the following week.
failure_condition: SPX 5-day forward returns are negative in > 55% of instances where RSAFS MoM exceeds 2.0%.

mechanism_narrative: Simultaneous growth in total nonfarm payrolls and a decline in the unemployment rate confirms robust labor market tightening, which supports corporate earnings growth and yields positive equity returns over one month.
failure_condition: Joint positive PAYEMS and negative UNRATE changes fail to produce positive 20-day SPX returns in > 60% of observations.

mechanism_narrative: During late-cycle expansions, a sustained decline in the industrial production index signals capacity constraints and supply bottlenecks that erode profit margins, causing negative S&P 500 forward returns over the next quarter.
failure_condition: 60-day forward returns remain positive or Rank IC < 0.02 during late-cycle expansion periods when INDPRO declines.

mechanism_narrative: When consumer price inflation rises faster than the federal funds rate, real interest rates turn negative, stimulating risk appetite and generating positive equity returns over the next month.
failure_condition: 20-day SPX returns are negative in > 50% of cases where CPIAUCSL growth outpaces FEDFUNDS growth.

mechanism_narrative: Higher real gross domestic product reflects stronger aggregate economic activity, which directly boosts corporate revenues and supports positive S&P 500 forward returns over the next quarter.
failure_condition: 60-day forward returns show negative mean or Rank IC < 0.03 across rolling 3-year windows when GDPC1 increases.

mechanism_narrative: A rapid rise in the unemployment rate exceeding 0.5 percentage points within three months signals an abrupt labor market deterioration, triggering recession fears and negative equity returns over the following week.
failure_condition: SPX 5-day forward returns are positive in > 65% of instances where UNRATE rises by more than 0.5pp over three months.

mechanism_narrative: Divergence between industrial production growth and housing starts indicates structural supply chain imbalances; when production rises while housing starts fall, it signals economic overheating that leads to negative equity returns over one month.
failure_condition: 20-day SPX returns are positive in > 55% of observations where INDPRO increases and HOUST decreases simultaneously.
